description: Sector overlay for South African nonprofit organisations, NGOs, community-based organisations, faith-based organisations, trusts, nonprofit companies, donor-funded bodies, and civil-society documentation.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for NGO. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African statutes, regulators, guidance, and sector sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Nonprofit Organisations Act, 1997 and NPO Directorate
South African Government NPO registration guidance
Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013
primary_sources.issuer: Information Regulator / Republic of South Africa

rules.title: NPO registration or founding-document evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for NPO registration, NPO number, founding document, constitution, trust deed, or nonprofit company evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Governance and office-bearer evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for board, trustees, office bearers, members, governance, meetings, or voting controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Non-distribution or public-purpose evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for public purpose, nonprofit purpose, non-distribution of income/property, or reasonable compensation clauses.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Financial accountability or donor-reporting evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for annual financial statements, accounting records, donor reporting, audit, or financial accountability evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Beneficiary, donor, or volunteer privacy evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for POPIA, privacy notice, beneficiary data, donor data, consent, or personal-information safeguards.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
